commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=37dbcadd10947fe8b8e101f71211b0047aca2f68)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes TUI launch and setup-finalize cleanup so parent stdin remains paused after an inherited-stdio TUI exits, with regression coverage for both paths.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests +13. Total +7 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main resumes stdin after the inherited-stdio child exits, and terminal-core documents that resuming stdin can keep stdin_open container parents alive. The PR body describes a PTY before/after reproduction, but the automated proof gate did not accept the submitted proof text.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The only code defect is mechanically fixable, but the external PR also needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof, so ClawSweeper should not take over the branch yet.

target.title: fix(tui): keep parent stdin paused after exit
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the parent CLI stdin stream paused after an inherited-stdio TUI child exits so `/exit` does not leave the parent process/container alive.
- Stop onboarding finalize cleanup from resuming stdin around the post-setup TUI handoff.
- Add focused regression coverage for the parent-stdin lifecycle.

## Verification

- Pre-fix clean-head PTY reproduction: parent script called `launchTuiCli`, child exited 0, output printed `launch returned`, then timed out waiting for parent exit with status 124.
- Post-fix PTY reproduction: same parent/child PTY proof printed `launch returned` and exited 0.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-launch.test.ts src/wizard/setup.finalize.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Bare/inherited-stdio TUI `/exit` can return from the child while the parent process remains alive because parent stdin was resumed.
Real environment tested: Local macOS PTY using `@lydell/node-pty`, preserving the PTY master open after the child TUI process exits.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a PTY reproduction that imports `src/tui/tui-launch.ts`, marks parent stdin flowing with `process.stdin.resume()`, launches an inherited-stdio child entry that exits 0 for `tui`, and waits up to 4 seconds for the parent to exit.
Evidence after fix: The post-fix reproduction printed `launch returned` and exited with status 0 instead of timing out.
Observed result after fix: Parent process exits after the TUI child exits, so the container/PTY is not kept alive by a resumed stdin stream.
What was not tested: Full Docker interactive package lane was not run; the focused PTY proof covers the inherited-stdio lifecycle that caused the observed Docker hang.
